name: Summer Vegetable Pickles
recipeIngredient: 3 small zucchini, cut into 5-inch by 1/2-inch batons
10 small red onions with tops, halved
recipeInstructions:
1. In a non-aluminum saucepan, stir together the vinegar, water, salt, peppercorns, garlic, fennel seeds, and sugar. Bring to a boil.
2. Cook for 2 minutes, then add fennel. Cook until just tender about 4 minutes, remove with a spider to a cool plate.
3. Add zucchini and cook until just tender about 1 minute, remove to a separate plate.
4. Add cauliflower and cook until tender about 3 minutes, remove to a cool plate.
5. Add red onions and cook until tender about 2 minutes, remove to a cool plate.
6. Return cooking liquid to a boil. Boil for 3 minutes.
7. Remove from heat and add oil and vinegar.
8. Divide vegetables among jars (1 or 2 quart jars work best, but any size will do). Cover with the cooking liquid. If there is not enough liquid, add a little white wine vinegar to cover.
9. Allow jars to cool, cover with lids, and refrigerate. Pickles will keep up to a month in the refrigerator.
